evals.prompt: Describe baseline accessibility requirements for a data chart.
evals.expected_output: Includes text alternative, data table, and summary insight.

evals.prompt: How should charts avoid color-only encoding?
evals.expected_output: Uses labels, patterns, or markers in addition to color.

evals.prompt: What interaction checks apply to interactive charts?
evals.expected_output: Keyboard access and focus visibility for controls and data points.

evals.prompt: A complex multi-series bar chart needs an accessible alternative. Map this to ARRM tasks, identify which roles own each task, and explain why this is a cross-role decision. Do not invent task IDs — use only real ARRM tasks.
evals.expected_output: Decomposes the problem into multiple ARRM tasks (at minimum IMG-008 or IMG-010 for text description, IMG-018 or CSS-009 for colour independence, TAB-series for data table, INP-004/INP-005 if interactive). Assigns each task to its actual primary owner from the ARRM CSV. Explains that no single role owns all tasks. Distinguishes minimum conformance from usability.
expectations: References at least 4 distinct real ARRM task IDs
Each ARRM task ID is mapped to its actual primary ownership role from the ARRM CSV
Identifies Content Authoring, Front-End Development, Visual Design, and/or UX Design as owners of different tasks
Explains why this requires cross-role coordination
Does not invent ARRM task IDs that do not exist in the CSV
Distinguishes between minimum WCAG conformance and usability improvements
